fact_nuggets.text: Static (Japanese: Static Electricity) is an Ability introduced in Generation III.

Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description MD R B Paralyzes attackers. MD T D S Paralyzes an attacker who lands a direct-contact attack. B S L Conq. Shock the target, with a potential to inflict paralysis. MD GtI May inflict the Paralysis status condition on an attacker when the Pokemon is hit by a direct attack. * When the Pokemon is hit with a direct attack like Tackle or Scratch, the attacker is sometimes afflicted with the Paralysis status condition! * S MD When the Pokemon is hit with a contact move like Tackle or Scratch, the attacker sometimes gets paralysis! MD RT DX When the Pokemon is hit with a contact move, like Tackle or Scratch, the attacker is sometimes afflicted with Paralysis.

Contents 1 Effect 1.1 In battle 1.1.1 Generation III 1.1.2 Generation IV onward 1.2 Outside of battle 1.2.1 Generation VIII 1.2.2 Generation IX Effect [ edit | edit source ] In battle [ edit | edit source ] Generation III [ edit | edit source ] When a Pokemon with this Ability is hit by a move that makes contact , there is a 1/3 chance that the attacking Pokemon will become paralyzed . This can affect Ground-type Pokemon. If a Pokemon with this Ability is hit by a multistrike move that makes contact, each hit has an independent chance to activate this Ability. Generation IV onward [ edit | edit source ] The chance for Static to paralyze is lowered to 30%. Outside of battle [ edit | edit source ] From Pokemon Emerald onward, if a Pokemon with Static is in the first place in the party (even if fainted ), then there is a 50% chance the game will force an encounter with an Electric-type Pokemon, if one is possible. As of Pokemon Sword and Shield , it shares this effect with Lightning Rod . Generation VIII [ edit | edit source ] In Pokemon Sword and Shield only, this chance is 51% instead. Generation IX [ edit | edit source ] In Pokemon Scarlet and Violet , this Ability has no effect outside of battle, with its former field function effectively being replaced by Meal Powers .

In battle [ edit | edit source ] Generation III [ edit | edit source ] When a Pokemon with this Ability is hit by a move that makes contact , there is a 1/3 chance that the attacking Pokemon will become paralyzed . This can affect Ground-type Pokemon. If a Pokemon with this Ability is hit by a multistrike move that makes contact, each hit has an independent chance to activate this Ability. Generation IV onward [ edit | edit source ] The chance for Static to paralyze is lowered to 30%.
Has a 30% chance of paralyzing attacking Pokemon on contact.
